content.content: {"result":"{\"emails\": [{\"email_id\": \"email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"william.white@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Hannah,\\n\\nI reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.\\n\\nPlease treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.\\n\\nYou may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.\\n\\nBest,\\nWilliam\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-11T20:45:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_028_monthly_close_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Draft Acme Cloud FP-2026-05 MAP package for review\", \"content\": \"Hi Jones,\\n\\nPackage looks green otherwise.\\n\\nPlease add one explicit sentence in the gross margin walk that the Datadog reclass is a presentation change, not an underlying operating improvement. Andrea will read the May move differently if that distinction is not stated plainly.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_028_monthly_close_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-06T15:42:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_024_monthly_close_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"margaret.sullivan@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Brookfield FP-2026-05 management accounts draft in progress\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nThe package looks complete to me. The Q2-wrap deductibility note is the right call and I would keep it in exactly as drafted, since it pre-empts the kind of later IRS-style question we would otherwise get during workpaper review.\\n\\nThat closes the loop on Andrea's earlier ask from my side. You have a clear green light to finalize the Brookfield FP-2026-05 management accounts package.\\n\\nBest,\\nMargaret\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_024_monthly_close_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-05T15:48:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0009\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition recommendation for exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d on BL-75810CD0FEE4\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nApproved to accept timing for FP-2026-05 on exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d tied to BL-75810CD0FEE4 and account 119000. Please leave the underlying exception referenced as-is for now rather than changing the disposition trail until we see whether the June rollforward lands.\\n\\nRevisit this at FP-2026-06 BD3. Please ask Anaya to flag in Slack whether the June engagement-hours rollforward is delivered or still missing so the coordination thread stays live and we can decide then whether the item clears or needs escalation.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_010_orphan_exception_0008\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-02T19:12:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.\\n\\nThe CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.\\n\\nMonday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-31T14:19:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_009_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"blue.evans@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Disposition approval request: exc_aade06f6129e43 for FP-2026-05 cash-payroll feed drop\", \"content\": \"Hi Blue,\\n\\nApproved. Accept-timing for FP-2026-05 is fine based on the 29 dropped rows, Ryan's note, George's precedent, and Hannah's downstream review.\\n\\nIf the June 2 retry does not clear, re-verify at FP-2026-06 BD3 and confirm whether anything changed in the feed behavior before we revisit disposition. Until then, leave exc_aade06f6129e43 in logged state.\\n\\nThanks,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-30T19:51:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_022_orphan_exception_0008\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue on 131000 due May 30\", \"content\": \"Hi Emily,\\n\\nPartner-sight noted on the $6,068.91 amount and Daniel's proposed accounting path.\\n\\nWhat stands out to me is not just the duplicate posting but the vendor-management gap behind the May 22 manual re-key. [{"email_id": "email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0004", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Escalation on VEN-010-514242 for acme_cloud", "content": "Hi Owen,

Following up here after checking with our side. The credit memo never arrived in our queue, and the AR contact who had been handling this left TimeLedger Nexus in late January 2026, which is part of why the trail went cold almost as soon as the invoice was issued.

I reopened the issue with our new AR lead last week and asked them to retrace the early-April 2026 follow-up on the disputed Phase-3 post-go-live tuning milestone. Based on what we can confirm right now, the cleanest workaround may be to release the undisputed $17,825 now and continue holding $6,650.25 pending the credit memo.

One other note: if this invoice is being surfaced through a 90-day SLA scan, that report likely needs a broader refresh because this one clearly fell outside the normal follow-up path once the contact changed over.

If that split-payment approach works on your side, I can keep pushing AR for the missing credit memo while you clear the undisputed portion.

Best,
Owen", "cc": ["anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-18T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}]

content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"32ab9d68b98c45e3a4ad79287d826e43\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"green.spatz@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nI am comfortable aligning to the updated review loop, but I need one point clarified. Can you confirm who gives the final approval on the graduate assignments, and whether that sign-off happens after the reviewer clears the edits or after a separate manager review?\\n\\nKind regards,\\nAnaya\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:52:11Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"079d9cff7e1a4203b3a71cb9fadcc381\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"margot.reyes@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Clint,\\n\\nI agree we need a tighter review loop, and I am escalating this to make sure the trainee files do not sit in limbo. If you are documenting the review standard for graduate assignments, please include who gives final sign-off and whether revised files go back to the same reviewer or directly to approval. That will help us keep the handoff clear on the current batch and avoid delays.\\n\\nThanks,\\nMargot\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"86818eedd4ad4b46bf2d08b4dc8ca978\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:43:42Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e7bb52403ed84f8fb19e2ccf6f0db07d\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"green.spatz@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nJust checking I have the workflow right: I send the draft to my reviewer, update it based on their notes, and then it goes to final approval. Can you confirm whether there is any extra step if the reviewer has only minor comments?\\n\\nRegards,\\nGreen\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"23a6325ea1024a20aaaee5b42aca7f62\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:38:27Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"23a6325ea1024a20aaaee5b42aca7f62\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"elita.moore@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nThat works for me. I just want to make sure I have the sequence right - after the first review, do I wait for the reviewer to confirm the changes before I forward it on, or is the revised draft enough to move ahead?\\n\\nThanks,\\nElita\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"86818eedd4ad4b46bf2d08b4dc8ca978\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:31:35Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"86818eedd4ad4b46bf2d08b4dc8ca978\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"clint.peterson@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel,\\n\\nThat approach sounds sensible from the team side. Before we lock it in, can you confirm whether the trainee should send the revised file back to the same reviewer for a second pass, or move straight to final approval once the comments are cleared?\\n\\nBest,\\nClint\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": \"e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:25:33Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"e121ddc4c25b4d7a9e3444de31ebfe91\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Blocker on trainee file completion review step\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nFollowing up on our review loop for the graduate assignments, I think we need a clearer control point before any trainee file is marked complete. At the moment, the handoff is still relying too much on informal checks, and that is creating avoidable rework. Could you please confirm whether you want us to escalate this to your manager and agree a formal review step for the next batch?\\n\\nRegards,\\nDaniel\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T19:20:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}]"}

input.subject: May FP-2026-05 WIP recognition status + residual parked per your instruction
input.content:
Hi Andrea,

Closing the loop on the May (FP-2026-05) WIP-to-revenue recognition before the management package goes up for review.

Recognition status
The $147,825 you lined up across the three service lines that crossed milestones in May is recognized and posted — JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057 (prepared 5/31, approved and posted same day). The total ties exactly to your engagement-stage completion review.

Residual parked
The separate $4,390.62 variance on the WIP unbilled-services anchor reconciliation (BL-75810CD0FEE4, account 119000) is left out of this cycle as next-period cleanup per your instruction. Hannah has approved accept-timing for May; we'll revisit at FP-2026-06 BD3 (2026-07-03), and Anaya will flag the June engagement-hours rollforward in the close thread.

One item to confirm before the package finalizes
The per-service-line split as posted differs from your stage schedule at the line level (the total is unchanged at $147,825):
  - 401 Audit & Assurance:        posted $89,400.00  vs  schedule $88,695.00
  - 402 Tax Preparation & Planning: posted $32,250.00  vs  schedule $36,956.25
  - 403 Advisory & Consulting:     posted $26,175.00  vs  schedule $22,173.75

Could you confirm which allocation is authoritative? If your stage schedule governs, I'll route a revenue-only reclass (DR 401 $705 / DR 403 $4,001.25 / CR 402 $4,706.25, net $0) through the normal close path so the service-line revenue in the management accounts agrees to your review before it goes out.

Thanks,
George

content.text:
George — I worked the May WIP recognition end to end and **three things change the read before this goes to Daniel.** I did *not* stage a new entry, because doing so would have double-booked revenue. Here's the full picture.

## ⚠️ The read changed — read this first

**1. The recognition is already posted and approved — not waiting to be staged.**
`JE-brookfield-FP-2026-05-0057` (je_53962aed96fe4b67), "May 2026 month-end WIP-to-revenue recognition per engagement-stage completion review," **$147,825**, prepared by you 5/31, **approved and posted the same day by Margaret Sullivan**. Staging a fresh $147,825 entry would have duplicated revenue, so I held off. If you still want something in front of Daniel, it should be a *correction*, not a re-post (see #2).

**2. The total ties to Andrea's stage schedule, but the per-service-line split does NOT.**
| Service line | Andrea's stage schedule | Posted JE-0057 | Variance |
|---|---|---|---|
| 401 Audit & Assurance | 88,695.00 | 89,400.00 | **+705.00** |
| 402 Tax Prep & Planning | 36,956.25 | 32,250.00 | **−4,706.25** |
| 403 Advisory & Consulting | 22,173.75 | 26,175.00 | **+4,001.25** |
| **Total** | **147,825.00** | **147,825.00** | 0.00 |

Total revenue is fine; **service-line revenue in the management package won't agree to Andrea's review.** If her schedule governs, the fix is a revenue-only reclass (DR 401 705 / DR 403 4,001.25 / CR 402 4,706.25, net $0) through the close path. I've asked Andrea to confirm which split is authoritative before the package finalizes.

**3. The residual's root cause doesn't survive contact with the source.** The recon/exception attribute the $4,390.62 to a "subledger feed retry / partial-success batch." The actual May **Time-and-WIP feed (`run_e33ed2561f2c46`) posted clean: 2,083/2,083 rows, 0 rejected, no retry.** The partial-batch story is Anaya's recollection, not what the feed log shows. The residual is real but its cause is unexplained — needs re-deriving before the BD3 revisit. (Edith may hold a source-doc reference.)

Also surfaced, which you hadn't seen: an **open, overdue reviewer note from Edith Banda** on the recon (`rn_564e65ce0d594f`): *"Foreign-currency revaluation entry referenced in the workbook — is this in scope for this period?"* — unanswered. 119000 is a domestic USD WIP account so FX reval shouldn't be in scope, but I didn't clear it on a guess — it needs Anaya/Edith on the record.

## What I did do (support is clean and parked)

- **Vault memo:** the existing support doc (`doc_42c851aed8fb40ab`) was a 114-byte empty stub. Replaced it with a complete, source-verified memo + engagement-stage schedule (restricted / AICPA_SQMS_7Y, linked to JE-0057) covering the posted entry, the tie-out and line-split variance, the residual disposition, the feed-run finding, and Edith's open note. Attached it as evidence to the recon.
- **Recon BL-75810CD0FEE4:** recorded the **accept-timing disposition per Hannah** (email 6/2) in the variance narrative, parked the $4,390.62 to FP-2026-06 BD3, noted the clean-feed caution. Left it **in_progress** (not submitted) and left **exception `exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d` referenced as-is** — no state change — exactly as Hannah asked.
- **Reminders:** deleted the orphan reminder on `exc_1ddfc978ce5a4d`; set a fresh follow-up due **2026-07-02** for the FP-2026-06 BD3 revisit (BD3 lock is 07-03), incl. asking Anaya to confirm the June rollforward and re-derive the root cause.
- **Slack:** posted a close-out note on the running thread (C005) — residual parked, accept-timing, BD3 revisit, Anaya to flag the June rollforward, plus the two open tie-outs.
- **Andrea:** emailed her — recognition status, residual parked per her instruction, and asked her to confirm the authoritative service-line split (with the reclass teed up if her schedule governs).

## My recommendation before you take it to Daniel
Don't move the management package until (a) Andrea confirms the 401/402/403 split — and if her schedule wins, route the net-$0 reclass through the close path; (b) the residual's real cause is established rather than carried forward as "partial feed"; and (c) Edith's review note is cleared on the record. The $147,825 total and the parked residual are solid; the service-line allocation is the one that would otherwise come back from Andrea after the fact.
